Here's an expanded timeline integrating key developments in medieval occult traditions with numeric dating:
| 100-400 | Physiologus | Alexandria | Christianized magical creatures (Phoenix, Basilisk) |
| 1100s | Aberdeen Bestiary | England | Dragons & mandrakes with necromantic associations |
| 1200s | De Bestiis et Aliis Rebus | France | Werewolves & salamanders tied to demonic forces |
| 800s | Liber Monstrorum | England | Dog-headed men (cynocephali) as necromantic hybrids |
| 800s | Kitab al-Khawass | Arabia | Talismanic magic attributed to Hermes Trismegistus |
| 800s | De Radiis Stellarum | Iraq | Al-Kindi's theory of celestial rays influencing earthly magic |
| 1200s | Shams al-Ma'arif | Algeria | Angel/demon magic with astrological timing |
Decanic Demons - 36 Egyptian star deities in Liber Hermetis
Lunar Mansions - 28 Arabic star stations for timing rituals
900s: Pope Sylvester II accused of necromancy for importing Arabic astral magic
1200s: Albertus Magnus writes on astrological magic in mineralogy
1300s: John of Morigny's Liber Visionum (angelic communications)
1400s: Book of Abramelin details planetary angel magic
1500s: John Dee's Enochian angelic language & astral travel
